2605.03929.title: PHALAR: Phasors for Learned Musical Audio Representations
2605.03929.paper_summary: Stem retrieval, the task of matching missing stems to a given audio submix, is a key challenge currently limited by models that discard temporal information. We introduce PHALAR, a contrastive framework achieving a relative accuracy increase of up to $\approx 70\%$ over the state-of-the-art while requiring $<50\%$ of the parameters and a 7$\times$ training speedup. By utilizing a Learned Spectral Pooling layer and a complex-valued head, PHALAR enforces pitch-equivariant and phase-equivariant biases. PHALAR establishes new retrieval state-of-the-art across MoisesDB, Slakh, and ChocoChorales, correlating significantly higher with human coherence judgment than semantic baselines. Finally, zero-shot beat tracking and linear chord probing confirm that PHALAR captures robust musical structures beyond the retrieval task.
2605.03929.paper_summary_zh: 幹線檢索，即將缺失的幹線與給定的音頻子混音進行匹配的任務，是一個關鍵挑戰，目前受到丟棄時間信息的模型的限制。我們介紹了PHALAR，一個對比框架，相較於最先進技術實現了高達$\approx 70\%$的相對準確率提升，同時所需參數少於$<50\%$，並且訓練速度提高了7倍。通過利用學習的頻譜池層和複數值頭，PHALAR強化了音高等變性和相位等變性偏差。PHALAR在MoisesDB、Slakh和ChocoChorales上建立了新的檢索最先進技術，與人類一致性評判的相關性顯著高於語義基準。最後，零樣本節拍跟踪和線性和弦探測確認PHALAR捕捉到了超越檢索任務的穩健音樂結構。
2605.03929.author: Davide Marincione et.al.
2605.03929.authors: Davide Marincione, Michele Mancusi, Giorgio Strano, Luca Cerovaz, Donato Crisostomi, Roberto Ribuoli, Emanuele Rodolà
